rulings.Case Title: HOWARD WEISS VS. SHANNON WILLS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, August 14, 2013, Line 15, Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Motion For Summary Judgment The motion for summary judgment is granted. The sign did not constitute a dangerous condition within the meaning of Gov't Code § 830(a) as a matter of law. A person exercising due care would not have crossed the unbroken double yellow line. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/LN

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MANUEL TOMANENG et al VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, DBA MUNI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs The Estate Of Manuel Tomaneng'S, Zen Mendoza'S, Oscar Tomaneng'S, And Arturo Tomaneng'S Motion For An Order Permitting Reproduction Of Photographs Of Decedent Manuel Tomaneng From The S.F. Medical Examiner
rulings.Rulings: Hearing set for Wednesday, August 14, 2013, Line 2: Plaintiffs The Estate of Manuel Tomaneng's, Zen Mendoza's, Oscar Tomaneng's, and Arturo Tomaneng's Motion for an Order Permitting Reproduction of Photographs of Decedent Manuel Tomaneng from The S.F. Medical Examiner Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is granted. The proposed order has been signed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CURTIS ANTHONY DAY VS. KARY WITT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, August 14, 2013, Line 8, DEFENDANT KARY WITT MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Denied as to the motion for summary judgment, or alternatively, summary adjudication. The Court concludes that the Complaint is insufficient as a matter of law and elects to treat the motion for summary judgment, or alternatively, summary adjudication as a motion for judgment on the pleadings. "[C]ourts have held that a defendant's motion for summary judgment necessarily includes a test of the sufficiency of the complaint, and the motion is treated as a motion for judgment on the pleadings." (People ex rel. Dept. of Transportation v. Outdoor Media Group (1993) 13 Cal.App.4th 1067, 1074.) In the Complaint, Plaintiff does not allege facts pertaining to the claim filing requirement of the Government Claims Act. (See Complaint; Reply, p. 1:13-15.) "It is well established that the pleadings determine the scope of relevant issues on a summary judgment motion." (Nieto v. Blue Shield of California Life & Health Ins. Co. (2010) 181 Cal.App.4th 60, 74.) The Court does not consider the evidence proffered in Plaintiff's opposition on this subject. Plaintiff is granted 5 days leave to amend, if he can do so in good faith, to allege compliance or excuse from compliance from the claim filing requirement of the Government Claims Act. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/LN

rulings.Case Title: JOSE MARTINEZ VS. JOHN C. JEW et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, August 14, 2013, Line 11, PLAINTIFF JOSE MARTINEZ MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD The motion is off calendar pursuant to CCP 1005 and CCP 12c. The moving party fails to provide a timely proof of service. The moving party mail-served the motion on July 24, 2013, and only provided 15 court day notice. CCP 1005 requires at least 16 court day notice and an additional 5 calendar days for mail service. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =302/LN
